item.title: Call for action on cold and flu products containing herb linked to anaphylaxis
item.description: Andrographis paniculata is a common ingredient in cold and flu supplements, but it may not be as low-risk as first thought.

item.title: Calls to protect culturally and linguistically diverse communities from 'anxiety-driven' NDIS cuts
item.description: Nidhi Shekaran is one of thousands of NDIS participants waiting to learn if their services will be impacted as part of sweeping government reforms to the scheme.

item.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby's family say they feel let down after missing her alleged murderer's court hearing
item.description: The family of a five-year-old girl who died after going missing from an Alice Springs town camp last month say they feel let down by the system after missing her alleged killer's first court date.

item.title: Talia Gibson recovers from dreadful second set to beat Martina Trevisan in Rome
item.description: Talia Gibson has made a fighting start on her debut on the clay of Rome, the Australian number two beating former world top-20 player Martina Trevisan to progress.

item.title: Fuel prices rising in the US as costs stabilise in Australia
item.description: Fuel prices in Australia have started to moderate just as costs have reached an almost four-year high in the United States.

item.title: Project Freedom has been paused, so will it lead to a diplomatic breakthrough or reignite war with Iran?
item.description: Iran had branded Project Freedom as "Project Deadlock" and now US president Donald Trump has placed the military's support mission in the Strait of Hormuz on hold.

item.title: Arsenal reach Champions League final by beating Atletico Madrid to close in on historic double
item.description: Arsenal reach the Champions League final for the second time in their history with a 1-0 home win over Atletico Madrid sealing a 2-1 aggregate victory in the semi-final.

item.title: What age should a child start swimming lessons?
item.description: Swimming ability is declining in Australia, and experts say knowing the right age to enrol your children in lessons — as well as what to do if you can't access them — is key to improving water safety.

item.title: Marco Rubio says Operation Epic Fury is 'over'
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Albury Hospital bed shortage hits hard ahead of Farrer by-election
item.description: A major hospital is facing a significant bed shortage in the electorate of Farrer, where residents will soon vote in a by-election.

item.title: Two Pacific Islander farm workers dead, one critical after Manjimup crash
item.description: The men died in a vehicle crash near Manjimup in the state's South West.

item.title: Nadia Hernández presents living archive of Venezuelan protest music at AGNSW
item.description: Unable to return to her homeland, Melbourne-based artist Nadia Hernández has reconnected with her culture through art.

item.title: Turkish YouTuber describes the situation onboard MV Hondius
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: US influencer onboard MV Hondius during hantavirus outbreak
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Freshwater turtles thriving only months after outback lake was on verge of drying up
item.description: Hundreds of turtles are thriving near an outback NSW town, only months after the lake where they live was on the verge of drying up.

item.title: Commercial fishing restrictions in Gulf St Vincent extends for stock recovery
item.description: One of South Australia's main fishing zones will remain closed for another year to allow stocks to recover from the algal bloom, sparking concerns from fishers over their future and warnings of price increases for consumers.

item.title: Afghanistan women's football player Mina Ahmadi says 'justice is served' after FIFA rule change
item.description: A FIFA rule change has allowed the Afghan women's football team to compete in official internationals for the first time since the Taliban took over in 2021. Mina Ahmadi says the victory is a "very emotional" one for the team.

item.title: More than $10 billion slated to boost fuel supplies and emergency stockpiles
item.description: More than $10 billion will be spent bolstering Australia's fuel security as the federal government attempts to shield the nation from the long-term consequences of the war in Iran.

item.title: Women's State of Origin II teams named with Rima Butler to debut for NSW as Queensland Maroons name squad
item.description: Queensland opts to name a squad, rather than a final line-up, for Women's State of Origin II, with NSW expected to hand a debut to powerful Roosters forward Rima Butler.

item.title: Australia to give $30 million to Fiji to combat rising fuel prices in Pacific nation
item.description: The Australian government says the funding will help Fiji combat rising fuel prices and support its role as a regional fuel hub.

item.title: Finland's world-leading defence tech could soon come to Australia, trade minister says
item.description: The company that provides much of Australia's satellite surveillance for real-time disaster management may also soon be sharing its defence technology, as Finland becomes the latest European country to seek closer ties on security.

item.title: Low level snow forecast for three states, as polar blast hits south-eastern Australia
item.description: A wintry mix of snow, icy temperatures, and showers is set to hit Australia's south-east, bringing a sharp end to the recent run of warm autumn weather.

item.title: Queensland police officers contest criminal charges over Mareeba watch house tasering
item.description: Two Queensland police officers are contesting the charges that relate to a 2024 incident at a regional watch house, arguing that investigators added things to the alleged victim's statement that he never said.

item.title: Monash IVF sued over embryo mix-up at Brisbane clinic
item.description: A couple is suing fertility giant Monash IVF for negligence over an embryo mix-up at its Brisbane clinic discovered last year.

item.title: Antisocial fan behaviour blamed as public World Cup screenings scrapped in Melbourne
item.description: The Melbourne Arts Precinct says the past actions of a "small number of people" at screenings in previous years has prompted the decision to not show matches this year.

item.title: Australian educational facilities impacted as 'criminal' hacks Canvas learning platform
item.description: The international learning management software Canvas, used by various universities and schools, was hacked on Saturday. One of the facilities impacted says some of its data has been accessed by a "criminal third party".

item.title: Perth suburbs blanketed in thick smoke haze after prescribed burn-off near Mundaring
item.description: Perth has been blanketed in a thick smoke haze, the result of a prescribed burn-off in the city's eastern fringes.

item.title: Wednesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Exercise physiologists campaign to 'axe the tax' on their services
item.description: The peak body for exercise physiologists says its members are being discriminated against with "unfair" taxes that other health services do not have to pay.

item.title: WHO pandemic director speaks on deadly hantavirus ship outbreak
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Mother issues warning after cake-decorating dust leaves toddler in induced coma
item.description: The mother of a Queensland toddler who is in an induced coma after inhaling a dust used for decorating cakes is calling for similar products to be pulled from shelves.

item.title: Will One Nation win the Farrer by-election?
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Australian government 'deciding factor' in departure of ISIS-linked families from Syria
item.description: The Syrian government says Australia was the ultimate decision-maker in the delay of a group of four women and nine children with links to Islamic State leaving Syria for Australia.

item.title: Pioneering CNN founder Ted Turner dead at 87
item.description: Turner revolutionised television news when he launched the world's first 24-hour news channel in 1980.

item.title: Iran reviews US proposal as Donald Trump says Operation Epic Fury could 'be at an end'
item.description: Iran reviews a new peace-deal proposal from the US, which President Donald Trump says could end the war, but an Iranian official describes it as an "American wish list".

item.title: From the outback to the ocean, an epic Australian road trip awaits
item.description: Whether you want to explore the red desert or picnic by a waterfall, an Aussie road trip awaits.

item.title: Put your Australian film knowledge to the test with this picture quiz
item.description: Think you know your Moulin Rouge from your Muriel's Wedding? Take our Australian movie quiz to prove you're the king of The Castle.

item.title: ISIS-linked families to arrive in Australia today with possibility of being arrested
item.description: Australian authorities are preparing for the arrival of 13 women and children linked to Islamic State fighters, expected to land in the country from Syria today.

item.title: Trump's words won't send the Albanese government back to the budget drawing board
item.description: It's clear that whatever this unpredictable US president says, the Albanese government isn't expecting a return to normal oil flows any time soon.

item.title: Richard Dawkins is asking the wrong question about AI
item.description: Is AI conscious? Richard Dawkins is asking the wrong question.

item.title: What if we used taxes or superannuation to control inflation, not just interest rates?
item.description: As the average mortgage borrower confronts another $100 a month on top of their already inflated repayments, lots of people are asking if there is a better way to contain inflation than simply raising interest rates. It turns out there might be.

item.title: The leader who overcame One Nation's first electoral shock warns against dismissing its voters
item.description: Nearly three decades after One Nation's shock breakthrough in Queensland, Pauline Hanson's party is again within reach of a significant political victory.

item.title: How big is Australia's generation gap? Here's what the evidence tells us
item.description: Next week's budget will have the generation gap at its heart. But nailing down exactly what that gap is can be difficult. Here's what the evidence says.

item.title: This island was once ruled by Australia. Now, it could become the world's newest country
item.description: A tropical archipelago just 1,500 kilometres from Australia is readying to become the world's newest country. But as Bougainville nears independence, memories of a bloody past are coming back to the surface.

item.title: What will become of the Victoria Barracks? Communities have their say
item.description: Veteran, housing, heritage and community organisations are urging the government to put public needs first as dozens of defence sites get prepped for sale.

item.title: Children in Melbourne's inner west at higher risk of hospital visits for asthma
item.description: There are renewed calls for government action after another study finds children in Melbourne's inner west are over-represented in asthma-related hospital visits.

item.title: More allegations of abuse levelled against charity founder Cody Schaeffer
item.description: More victims of former Order of Australia recipient Cody Schaeffer have spoken to the ABC about his actions.

item.title: Fresh strikes in southern Lebanon, calls for Australia to improve self-reliance — as it happened
item.description: Local media reports Israeli strikes in a southern Lebanese town that has not been targeted in several weeks. Here's how the day unfolded on our Iran war blog.

item.title: Paddock-to-plate revival as farmers grapple with surging food freight costs
item.description: As the cost of each kilometre food travels explodes, growers and consumers are looking for ways to shorten the supply chain.

item.title: Algal bloom 'uncertainty' persists despite reducing levels of karenia at coastal SA tourist town
item.description: The damage caused by South Australia's harmful algal bloom is continuing to be documented on the Yorke Peninsula, even as government water testing shows reduced levels of karenia across most of the coastline.

item.title: Exit 8 horror film director Genki Kawamura wants people to break out of the loop
item.description: The creator of the minimalist horror film hopes it inspires viewers to stop doomscrolling and take a look at the world around them.

item.title: NSW gun sales plummet amid national buyback uncertainty
item.description: Gun sales are plummeting across NSW, with the industry calling for a proposed buyback to be scrapped. But a public health expert says safety must take priority over retail concerns.

item.title: Alice Springs riot another flashpoint for town trying to heal. What will come next?
item.description: The riot in Alice Springs was yet another flashpoint for the small town trying to heal from heartbreak. So what does the future hold for Central Australia?

item.title: Locust outbreak 'unlikely' in South Australia but preparations continue ahead of forecast El Niño
item.description: Swarms of locusts in South Australia are not likely to reach outbreak levels, according to experts, with potential El Niño weather conditions forecast.

item.title: Cost of living keeps Victorians awake at night — and there are still 200 sleeps until the election
item.description: The state budget landed on the same day the Reserve Bank once again jacked up interest rates. ABC Melbourne listeners say the cost of living is keeping them awake at night.

item.title: Mother's Day high tea brings connection for women who have experienced pregnancy and infant loss
item.description: High tea events around the country are helping women of all ages and cultures feel connected and heard as they grieve their pregnancy and infant loss.

item.title: Pat Cummins stars for Hyderabad Sunrisers, Cooper Connolly's IPL ton in vain
item.description: Man of the match Pat Cummins inspires Hyderabad to victory, while also enjoying a young Aussie opponent hitting a century.

item.title: Outback college opens Gulf of Carpentaria's only senior school
item.description: For more than a century, outback students have had to leave home to finish school. Now, a remote Queensland school is changing that.

item.title: Cyclone Narelle pushes housing shortages deeper into crisis in Exmouth
item.description: Exmouth was already part of the national housing shortage, but since Cyclone Narelle, the problem has reached a crisis point.

item.title: Facing a record fifth Commonwealth Games, Angie Ballard leads Australia's Para athletics squad for Glasgow 2026
item.description: When it comes to Australian Para sporting legends, few names match it with Angie Ballard, who will lead the Para athletics contingent to a historic fifth Commonwealth Games at Glasgow in July.

item.title: Push to double Labor's $10 billion housing fund as demand soars for social housing
item.description: Australia's peak body for homelessness is leading calls for the federal government to pour at least $10 billion extra into social and affordable housing.

item.title: Paris Saint-Germain see off Bayern Munich in gripping Champions League semifinal to set Arsenal final date
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain will face Arsenal in the Champions League final after a 1-1 second-leg draw at Bayern Munich sealed the reigning champions' passage through.

item.title: South West WA commercial fishermen vow to challenge demersal ban
item.description: The owners of a Bunbury seafood business raise money to fund a legal challenge against the WA government's controversial demersal fishing ban.

item.title: Cold blast in Victoria brings snow to alpine peaks a month out from ski season opening
item.description: Snow has fallen on Victoria's higher peaks overnight, as below-zero temperatures blast the state.

item.title: Mary Fowler's Manchester City wins WSL title ahead of Arsenal and Matildas teammates
item.description: Australian star Mary Fowler becomes an English football champion with Manchester City after Arsenal blow their WSL title hopes with a draw at Brighton.

item.title: F1 could go back to V8 engines, but the hybrid investment means electric power must be considered
item.description: A bold vision of V8 engines has swept Formula 1, but the billions of dollars invested into hybrid technology means any view of the future must still consider electric power in one form or another.

item.title: Aftermath of an Israeli strike on a Beirut building
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Canvas data breach leaves education providers scrambling as student data compromised
item.description: Education institutions around the country are scrambling to respond to a global data breach that has affected Australian universities, TAFE and state schools in Queensland.

item.title: Federation Square decision not to screen 2026 FIFA World Cup overturned
item.description: Screenings of the FIFA World Cup will go ahead at Federation Square later this year after the Victorian premier overturned an unpopular decision to not host fans at the precinct.

item.title: Lawyer says David Warner will 'accept responsibility for drink-driving'
item.description: Former Australian Test cricketer David Warner will accept responsibility for a "reckless", "foolish" and "wrong" decision that resulted in a mid-range drink driving charge, his lawyer says.

item.title: Ahead of his record game for North Queensland, Jason Taumalolo is still forging new legends
item.description: As he prepares to become the most capped player in North Queensland history, Cowboys veteran Jason Taumalolo has come back from the brink to forge one last legend in his glittering career.

item.title: Annie Gas Field Project approved for waters near Twelve Apostles in Otway Basin
item.description: The gas field off the south-west coast of Victoria could provide up to 4 per cent of the east coast's energy supplies.

item.title: India's Narendra Modi emerges stronger after West Bengal win, vowing development and stability
item.description: Mamata Banerjee was once considered a threat to India's Narendra Modi. But now, her historic defeat due to "misrule" has the Indian Opposition in tatters.

item.title: Leaders call for child protection reviews ahead of Kumanjayi Little Baby vigils
item.description: As memorials for Kumanjayi Little Baby are held across the country, leaders call for a review of the child protection system to ensure the safety of young people is prioritised.

item.title: National resilience forum warned increasing Australia's self-reliance would come at a hefty price
item.description: With the war in the Middle East focusing attention on fuel reserves and Australia's self-reliance, experts warn there is no quick, or cheap, fix to reducing dependence on global supply chains.

item.title: Walbunja country artists paint eco-friendly surfboards
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: British PM Sir Keir Starmer and Labour bracing for voter backlash at local elections
item.description: They might be local elections, but the results of thousands of votes being held on Thursday around Britain are likely to have major ramifications for the national government.

item.title: Abusive customers to be banned from South Australian stores under new laws
item.description: Aggressive and violent customers could be barred from supermarket chains and shopping centres under sweeping South Australian laws that have come into effect this week.

item.title: Worst-ever mouse plague in Morawa, WA, leaves streets covered in mice
item.description: A severe mouse plague in the farming town of Morawa overwhelms homes, businesses and farms, prompting urgent calls for government assistance amid growing health concerns and rising economic losses.

item.title: ATSB finds pilot killed in helicopter crash on Qld cattle station was not equipped for night flight
item.description: An ATSB investigation into a fatal helicopter crash found the pilot was flying after sunset without the correct qualifications or equipment, prompting new safety procedures at the remote cattle station where he was working.

item.title: Gas export restrictions and better domestic prices promised under new east coast reservation policy
item.description: A new east coast gas reserve will "drive down" prices, according to the federal government and ensure Australia is no longer "hostage" to international markets.

item.title: ARN execs face investor backlash over axed $200m Kyle and Jackie O contracts
item.description: ARN Media reveals "brand safety" concerns over the Kyle and Jackie O Show have cost the company tens of millions in advertising revenue.

item.title: How the so-called 'ISIS brides' made their way from Syria to Australia
item.description: Advocates and the former Home Affairs boss say the return of another wave of so-called 'ISIS brides' was "inevitable". But a hands-off approach means Labor lost control of the narrative.

item.title: Brush turkey rescue
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Brush turkey rescued six months after being shot with arrow on NSW Central Coast
item.description: A maimed brush turkey that was shot with a bow and arrow on the NSW Central Coast is rescued after wandering in to a resident's kitchen.

item.title: WA Budget 2026 delivers cash for housing, fuel handout as $2.4 billion surplus forecast
item.description: The WA government delivers a big-spending budget, focused on housing, health and cost of living relief including a $100 fuel handout, as a $2.4 billion surplus is unveiled. Here's what you need to know.

item.title: NSW Electoral Commission reopens investigation into Chris Minns political donations allegations
item.description: The NSW election watchdog has quietly reopened an investigation into allegations that NSW Premier Chris Minns conspired to evade state donations laws.

item.title: DFAT says it has not been talking with Syrian officials about return of 'ISIS brides'
item.description: The Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade says it has not had any contact with Syrian officials about the return of a group of Australian women and children with links to the Islamic State terrorist group.

item.title: Solomon Islands to get new leader after Jeremiah Manele voted out as prime minister
item.description: Solomon Islands is set to have a new leader next week, with Jeremiah Manele voted out as prime minister after a fiery debate in parliament.

item.title: Adelaide Crows to feed off returning skipper Jordan Dawson's energy
item.description: Adelaide expects to feed off Jordan Dawson's energy when the club captain returns for a rare trip to the MCG to take on Richmond.

item.title: Liberals and One Nation scuffle, Albanese tries ACDC diplomacy
item.description: The heat is on in Farrer ahead of Saturday's by-election while in Canberra the prime minister has gone above and beyond with a gift for Japan's leader.

item.title: Federal government commits $5.9 million to illicit tobacco reform in the Northern Territory
item.description: The federal government has given $5.9 million to the Northern Territory to "shut down sleazy, ridiculous shops selling illegal tobacco".

item.title: Kingborough Deputy Mayor Clare Glade-Wright wins Huon Legislative Council seat
item.description: Clare Glade-Wright unseats fellow independent Dean Harriss in the southern Tasmanian upper house seat of Huon.

item.title: Three ISIS-linked women arrested facing mix of terrorism and slavery charges
item.description: A group of Australian women and children with links to former ISIS fighters have begun arriving in Australia, nearly two weeks after leaving a refugee camp in Syria.

item.title: NSW woman seeks damages from health district over amputated lower leg
item.description: The woman says her leg was amputated despite it being "salvageable", but doctors argue the amputation was necessary to save her life.

item.title: Brisbane Lions player Koby Evans gets four-week ban for homophobic slur
item.description: Brisbane Lions player Koby Evans is banned for four games after being found guilty of conduct unbecoming for using a homophobic slur against an opponent in the VFL.

item.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby remembered at vigils across the country
item.description: Vigils have been held across the country in memory of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby, who was allegedly murdered in Alice Springs.

item.title: Dogs coach says they're 'an easy target' for referees after fourth straight loss
item.description: The Dolphins recover from a rough start to hand the Bulldogs a fourth straight loss as coach Cameron Ciraldo bemoans decisions made by his players and the referees.

item.title: Townsville Hospital doctors write to premier demanding cultural change
item.description: More than 100 senior clinicians from a major regional hospital raise concerns in a letter to the state government about hospital culture.

item.title: Ted Turner's infamous feud with Rupert Murdoch began in a Hobart boathouse
item.description: The two media moguls shared a bitter rivalry for decades after some drunken comments at a Hobart boathouse. Here's how their rivalry unfolded through the years.

item.title: World Health Organization warns hantavirus cases could rise as efforts intensify to prevent spread
item.description: A number of countries are scrambling to trace people who disembarked from an Atlantic cruise struck by a deadly virus outbreak.

item.title: Concerns for competency of elderly woman rescued in WA outback then arrested
item.description: Concerns have been raised about the competency of an elderly Indigenous woman facing a charge of unlawful wounding from 2024, after she was rescued and then arrested by police in Western Australia's outback this week.

item.title: Thursday finance with Ian Verrender
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Hawthorn 'taught a bit of a lesson' after Fremantle's fourth-term blitz
item.description: Fremantle overcomes a 15-point deficit to beat Hawthorn thanks to a dominant final quarter.

item.title: ISIS-linked families arriving at Melbourne airport
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Marco Rubio visits the Vatican after Donald Trump's social media attacks on Pope Leo XIV
item.description: The secretary of state was seeking to mend fences after US President Donald Trump's social media broadsides against the pope for his opposition to the Iran war.

item.title: Two men, including UK immigration officer, guilty of spying on politicians and activists for China
item.description: Two British Chinese nationals have been found guilty of spying for China, using UK police systems to gather information.

item.title: Ukraine launches second-biggest drone attack on Russia amid rows over Victory Day ceasefire
item.description: The Kremlin is taking extraordinary measures ahead of Russia's marking of the defeat of Nazi Germany, as Ukraine launches its second-biggest attack of the war so far.

item.title: Comedian Urzila Carlson on the benefits of a work husband and getting stage fright
item.description: Urzila Carlson's start in comedy was an accident, but now the stand-up regular is bringing sketch comedy to our TV screens.

item.title: ABC weekly news quiz: A stranded ship, an underwhelming space show and a Met movie moment
item.description: Don't bother looking up at the sky, it won't be worth it. Instead, try your luck testing your news knowledge from the past week.

item.title: After years in limbo in Syria, ISIS-linked families arrive in Australia to more uncertainty
item.description: When a group of so-called ISIS brides boarded a flight from Doha to Melbourne, they took a leap of faith, unsure of what fate awaited them at the end of their journey. But they said they were willing to face arrest for the freedom of their children.

item.title: Housing downturn possible as demand softens and rates kick in, Cotality warns
item.description: The tight supply conditions that have underpinned the national housing market in recent years are starting to ease, according to property data firm Cotality.

item.title: What negative gearing and CGT changes will mean for rental prices
item.description: With changes to negative gearing and capital gains tax coming in this year's federal budget, we spoke to three experts who explained how this will impact rental prices.

item.title: Tech entrepreneurs warn removing CGT discount will be 'disastrous' for startups and small business
item.description: Some entrepreneurs argue any changes to reduce the capital gains tax discount should carve out the startup sector. However, tax experts warn this would create too much complexity in an already complicated tax system.

item.title: The first American pope, Leo XIV, has stayed steady in a year defined by chaos
item.description: Foreign leaders and lobbyists thought they would write the next chapter in Catholic history. They failed and Leo is proving to be the pope that some of them feared.

item.title: Economists say trusts help people pay less tax, but stopping that could be difficult
item.description: Tuesday's federal budget is set to contain changes to the rules for trust funds, which economists say are a popular vehicle for tax planning.

item.title: Farrer by-election result will set off a willy-willy of spin. The budget already has
item.description: The run-up to a budget always sees a degree of chaos. But this year it has looked like one of those willy-willies that blow up in outback Australia when the wind stirs a storm of dust.

item.title: Three women face crimes against humanity and terrorism charges after allegedly travelling to ISIS-controlled Syria
item.description: The three women have arrived in Australia nearly two weeks after leaving a refugee camp in Syria.

item.title: How Russia came to dominate global nuclear energy and build a network of dependence
item.description: Russia is building nuclear power plants across the globe, and it's helping it build a network of dependence.

item.title: Criticism of proposed changes to superannuation laws that allow perpetrators to financially benefit
item.description: The federal government is reviewing superannuation laws that could see retirement savings paid to domestic violence perpetrators. But industry experts are worried the draft policy reform could only make matters worse.

item.title: In-home childcare sector faces collapse as government fails to extend wage subsidies for educators
item.description: The government is accused of breaking its promise of cheaper child care, with prices rising for 810 vulnerable families.

item.title: Department plans decision on Woodside's Browse gas project as soon as next month
item.description: Woodside's proposal to open up Australia's largest untapped gas resource, the Browse Basin, could be handed to the environment minister for a final decision as soon as next month.

item.title: Queensland shivers through coldest morning of the year
item.description: A chilly southern air mass has most of southern Queensland shivering through the coldest morning of the year so far.

item.title: Australian drivers Paige Raddatz and Joanne Ciconte make Kyojo Cup debut
item.description: The next chapter for two promising Australian motor racing talents begins this weekend as Japan's Kyojo Cup commences its new season.

item.title: Smashed by 1,000 points in four matches, these Dogs are refusing to lie down
item.description: The Dennington Dogs are a 117-year-old club that some fear might not make it to 118. But there's more to the team's beltings this season than meets the eye.

item.title: French Open boycott fears grow as Jannik Sinner says players do not feel respected
item.description: The world's leading tennis players do not feel respected, Jannik Sinner says, as fears grow of the French Open being boycotted.

item.title: Labor's absence in Farrer by-election could reshape poll outcome
item.description: The Labor Party has opted not to run a candidate in tomorrow's Farrer by-election, which will result in a redistribution of votes as traditional Labor supporters are forced to look elsewhere.

item.title: NSW councils call for 'GP guarantee' to address regional health crisis
item.description: The peak body for NSW councils has a six-point plan to help fix the state's regional health crisis.

item.title: International Olympic Committee lifts restrictions on Belarusian athletes
item.description: The International Olympic Committee has lifted its restrictions on Belarusian athletes competing under their own flag, although World Athletics has said it will keep its ban in place.

item.title: Aboriginal dance company brings true story of Stolen Generations survivor to stage
item.description: A dance that tells the story of a displaced Aboriginal man's struggle with identity has travelled the country and is now showing at the Sydney Opera House.

item.title: Mitchell Marsh hits rapid IPL century as Lucknow Super Giants record third win of season
item.description: Mitchell Marsh smashed a brutal century to help Lucknow Super Giants win just their third match of the IPL season as Virat Kohli fails in Royal Challengers Bengaluru's chase.

item.title: Federal budget to include $64b in reprioritisations and savings — as it happened
item.description: The federal government says the budget will be better off after it found $64 billion in savings and reprioritisations. Take a look back at the day's coverage below.

item.title: ISIS-linked woman and children arrive at Melbourne Airport
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: ASX falls the furthest in seven weeks in near-$50b wipeout
item.description: The Australian share market closes sharply lower, experiencing the biggest one-day fall in seven weeks and a near-$50 billion wipeout as oil prices rose on reports of a fresh escalation of Middle East tensions.

item.title: Rising waste costs prompt rethink of rubbish collection around Australia
item.description: How councils collect rubbish is changing as the cost of disposing of waste skyrockets and they scramble to meet environmental targets.

item.title: Real Madrid dressing room spat sees Federico Valverde taken to hospital after clash with Aurélien Tchouaméni
item.description: Teammates Federico Valverde and Aurélien Tchouaméni are involved in an altercation at Real Madrid training that left skipper Valverde in hospital with a head trauma.

item.title: US expects Iranian response on proposal to end war within hours
item.description: Iran and the US exchanged fire after weeks of relative calm in the Middle East, with each side claiming the other initiated the violence.

item.title: NBA Playoffs: LeBron and Embiid's resurgence and Knicks title shot among five first-round lessons
item.description: LeBron James shows you can never count out a team he is on, even at 41, as the first round of the playoffs wraps up.

item.title: Queensland Audit Office finds poor bookkeeping responsible for billion dollars in 'found assets'
item.description: A Queensland Audit Office report finds a $1.34 billion discrepancy between what appeared on council books and maps.

item.title: Cameron Smith given 'every assurance' LIV will continue despite funding issues
item.description: Cameron Smith says the LIV golf league will continue past 2026 despite funding issues.

item.title: Do you have a mother wound? Here's how to heal it
item.description: It's the yearning ache that comes from reckoning with the imperfect human mother we get, against the idealised fantasy version we wish for.

item.title: United States trade court rules against Trump's 10 per cent tariff rate
item.description: A United States trade court rules against President Donald Trump's universal 10 per cent tariff rate, dealing another blow to his signature economic policy.

item.title: WA Treasurer Rita Saffioti walks budget tightrope amid fears of global conditions worsening
item.description: Even with another eye-watering surplus, the uber-wealthy Western Australian government can't deliver its population one of the biggest things people want: certainty in an uncertain world.

item.title: Joint NSW–Victoria police operation targets outlaw motorcycle gangs and organised crime
item.description: Police from Victoria and NSW spend a week on the Victoria–NSW border targeting outlaw motorcycle gangs and organised crime.

item.title: Magnetic South land clearing found not to have breached national environmental law
item.description: The federal government says Magnetic South's land clearing last year was not illegal, but a conservation group says FOI documents raise questions over the validity of the decision.

item.title: Cockatoo removed from Orange's Cook Park aviary amid welfare concerns
item.description: Orange City Council says it is meeting all the requirements of its licence to operate the Cook Park aviary but concerned locals say it is time the enclosure was closed for good.

item.title: Giro d'Italia 2026: How to watch the Aussie cyclists targeting success on Italian roads
item.description: The 109th Giro d'Italia gets underway in Bulgaria on Friday, with 12 Aussies competing for glory in the first grand tour of the 2026 season. Here's all you need to know.

item.title: What is known about the IS-linked families that arrived in Australia
item.description: Women and children linked to the Islamic State terrorist group have arrived in Melbourne and Sydney. Here's who they are and what awaits them.

item.title: Today in History, May 8: Beyoncé makes her acting debut in Carmen: A Hip Hopera
item.description: Carmen: A Hip Hopera starred superstar Beyonce in her acting debut. It's been 25 years since the now cult classic musical drama aired on MTV in the United States.

item.title: Statement from mother of Kumanjayi Little Baby read at Alice Springs vigil, as nation mourns five-year-old girl
item.description: At a vigil held in Alice Springs last night for Kumanjayi Little Baby, a statement was read on behalf of her mother who called for the community to remember her five-year-old child as a "pretty girl in pink" who was "truly loved" by her family.

item.title: Julie Bishop resigns as Australian National University chancellor
item.description: The former foreign minister had previously rejected calls from university staff and students to resign.

item.title: Boral withdraws Reedy Creek quarry proposal after months of community opposition
item.description: After months of protests and community backlash, Boral has officially withdrawn its development application for the proposed Reedy Creek quarry. Local residents and campaigners are celebrating the major turnaround

item.title: Jewish teacher feared for his life marching in Sydney Mardi Gras parade, royal commission hears
item.description: The man told the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion he was embraced by the LGBTQIA+ community when he came out as gay as a teenager, but the experience was markedly different when he told loved ones of his religious conversion.

item.title: Battle for Farrer sees major parties on the outer as voters switch loyalties
item.description: The Coalition has held the seat of Farrer for more than 70 years, but voters sense that could change after Saturday's by-election.

item.title: Link Airways suspends Shellharbour flights as fuel costs soar and demand slumps
item.description: Link Airways will suspend its Shellharbour–Melbourne and Shellharbour–Brisbane services from May 20, saying it is losing tens of thousands of dollars a day as fuel costs rise and passenger numbers fall.

item.title: Richard Lewer wins Archibald Prize 2026 for portrait of Pitjantjatjara elder Iluwanti Ken
item.description: The Melbourne-based artist painted Pitjantjatjara elder Iluwanti Ken, who is known for her large-scale drawings and was also a finalist in this year's Wynne Prize.

item.title: What we know about renewed fighting between the US and Iran
item.description: The US and Iran both blame each other for initiating renewed attacks as tensions in the Strait of Hormuz ratchet up, threatening the ceasefire.

item.title: Charges dropped against AFL players Dion Prestia and Steven May over alleged brawl
item.description: Prosecutors have dropped all charges against current and former footballers Dion Prestia and Steven May over an alleged brawl in Sorrento on Victoria's Mornington Peninsula on Boxing Day in 2024.

item.title: Aiva Anagnostiadis to race BMW in GT4 Italy Series with Promodrive
item.description: Teenager Aiva Anagnostiadis, a rising star of Australian motorsport, is hoping to leave a difficult end to 2025 in her rear view as she prepares for her newest challenge in Italy.

item.title: China sentences former defence ministers to death with reprieves after purge
item.description: Two former defence ministers have received their sentences after a long-running corruption crackdown targeting China's military.

item.title: Police advised to disable bluetooth on Axon body-worn cameras to avoid detection by criminals
item.description: Thousands of police officers have been advised to disable the bluetooth on their Axon body-worn cameras during raids and undercover jobs to avoid being detected and tracked, after Four Corners exposed a security flaw with the devices.

item.title: Coalition plans to cut migration in move Angus Taylor vows will ease housing crisis
item.description: The Coalition will link overseas migration to housing supply as part of its migration strategy, which would in effect slash the number of migrants allowed into the country.

item.title: Two teenagers who acted in 'shore party' for drug importers jailed
item.description: The pair, now aged 19, were sentenced with 18 months and two years in prison respectively.

item.title: How to work out what concessions you are eligible for from the 2026/27 WA budget
item.description: Some WA families could receive thousands of dollars from this year's cost-of-living relief measures in the state budget. Here's how to work out what you are eligible for.

item.title: Sports Minister Anika Wells repays $10,000 in travel costs after audit
item.description: An audit of Communications and Sports Minister Anika Wells's travel by the Independent Parliamentary Expenses Authority found four breaches, but cleared her controversial trips to New York and Thredbo.

item.title: Bega's costs are up 10pc and customers will pay 'a large chunk' of it
item.description: One of Australia's biggest dairy manufacturers, Bega, is warning about soaring food inflation, as the war in the Middle East disrupts global supply chains.

item.title: Eruption of Mount Dukono in Indonesia kills three people
item.description: Mount Dukono erupted on Friday morning, local time, sending plumes of ash some 10 kilometres into the sky.

item.title: Why there's no end in sight for Kezie Apps, Origin's ageless wonder
item.description: Kezie Apps will be the oldest State of Origin player across both states and the men's and women's series this year, but the 35-year-old Blues stalwart shows no signs of slowing down.

item.title: Inquest told Queensland prisoner Glen Francis died weeks after pain dismissed as ‘drug-seeking behaviour’
item.description: A Queensland prisoner died in prison of a rare bacterial condition after his calls for medical help went unanswered for weeks, a medical expert has told a coronial inquest.

item.title: Closing statements heard in Charlotte MacInnes's defamation case against Rebel Wilson
item.description: Final arguments in the defamation trial between Charlotte MacInnes and Rebel Wilson have heard claims of a suspicious career jump to "terrible, terrible" allegations.

item.title: Chinese oil tanker's crew raises safety concerns after attack near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The chief engineer of a Chinese-owned oil tanker attacked near the Strait of Hormuz says it is "still unclear" who fired on the vessel.

item.title: Tens of thousands of students and teachers unable to access QLearn following cybersecurity breach
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of students have been unable to access schoolwork or submit assessments as access to Canvas has been paused.

item.title: NT taxpayers on the hook for $75m if Beetaloo Basin fracking project defaults
item.description: Northern Territory taxpayers will be hit with a $75 million bill if Tamboran Resources, one of the key Beetaloo Basin fracking companies, is unable to repay its lenders.

item.title: Russia's highest-profile return to sport to take place at World Aquatics Water Polo World Cup finals in Sydney in July
item.description: Russia will be given its highest profile platform since its sporting exile at the World Aquatics Water Polo World Cup finals in Sydney in July this year.

item.title: 'We don't want the penalty': Moses wins the game but doesn't end it
item.description: Mitchell Moses stuns the Cowboys in a bizarre golden-point finish, after Parramatta crosses twice in the final six minutes of normal time.

item.title: Hobart man fined for performing Nazi salute in front of police outside waterfront bar
item.description: The man pleaded guilty to four charges, with a magistrate describing the Nazi gesture charge as a very serious offence.

item.title: SA Premier Peter Malinauskas 'concerned' as Whyalla steelworks' blast furnace remains shut
item.description: Whyalla steelworkers are struggling to get the city's blast furnace producing iron again, with SA Premier Peter Malinauskas saying he is "very concerned" about the situation.

item.title: NT government backbencher Tanzil Rahman criticises 'bloated and ineffective' public service
item.description: A Northern Territory government backbencher has criticised the "giant, bloated and ineffective" NT public service, saying its current size is not feasible.

item.title: Friday Finance with David Chau
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: 'I don't coach for my job': Voss defiant after latest loss, Dogs edge Port
item.description: Carlton coach Michael Voss is frustrated after a close loss to Brisbane, but denies he is coaching for his job, while the Western Bulldogs hold off Port Adelaide despite being held to one goal in the second half.

item.title: Independent review finds five adverse findings against former Australian National University council members
item.description: An independent review into 36 allegations concerning the Australian National University has made a series of adverse findings against former council members.

item.title: Japan confirms first fatal bear attack this year with two more suspected
item.description: Japanese authorities say a 55-year-old woman found dead last month was killed by a bear. It is the first confirmed fatal bear attack for 2026 following a record 13 deaths last year.

item.title: Budget night productivity package to include migration overhaul and red tape drive
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers says Australia's productivity problem will take a long time to fix, but a $10 billion annual saving on business compliance costs is a start.

item.title: Hostages freed after German bank robbery, suspects on run
item.description: Two hostages have been freed unhurt after being held in a bank in the German town of Sinzig, but despite heavily-armed police surrounding the bank, the suspects were able to escape.

item.title: British Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer refuses to step down despite woeful local election results in England, Wales and Scotland
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer's Labour party has lost hundreds of seats in the local government election in England and has admitted defeat in Wales - which was previously Labour heartland.

item.title: Today in History, May 9: The 'magnificent' opening of Australia's first federal parliament
item.description: More than 12,000 people packed into Melbourne's Royal Exhibition Building to witness the "pomp and ceremony" of a "magnificent" occasion in the country's history.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of North by Northwest, Icarus, dog breeds and Gotye vs Carly Rae Jepsen
item.description: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

item.title: Australian author Ellena Savage considers our ecological disinheritance in debut novel, The Ruiners
item.description: Ellena Savage's debut essay collection, Blueberries, was a surprise hit. In her new novel, The Ruiners, she takes on Charles Dickens and the illegal waste trade.

item.title: Some people will hate Tuesday's federal budget but Jim Chalmers doesn't mind
item.description: For once, the budget won't be a dull one. Jim Chalmers's tax reforms will create a stir and some question whether they will close the generation gap, but the treasurer is projecting a relaxed figure.

item.title: The two sides of India's $130 billion wedding obsession
item.description: Foreign Correspondent crosses India to see how marriage has become big business.

item.title: Billions in investment waiting for biofuel policy certainty, super investors say
item.description: Billions of dollars worth of investment will flow if the federal government mandates a percentage of biofuels be added to the nation's fuel supply, major investors say.

item.title: Military parades and promises of peace ring hollow when words lose their meaning
item.description: Displays of military strength and perpetual promises of peace do little to mask the reality that major powers aren't always able to control the wars they helped create.

item.title: Tough talk on ISIS-linked families won't fix Australia's terrorism problem
item.description: The political energy spent on the "ISIS brides" has not been matched by serious attention to the deficiencies in Australia's counterterrorism architecture.

item.title: EVs transcend politics as petro-vulnerability supercharges pragmatism
item.description: The climate war is being won quietly, with nearly three in four One Nation voters eager to buy an EV.

item.title: Voters in Farrer welcome by-election day after protracted campaign
item.description: After three months of intense campaigning, voters in the NSW electorate say whoever wins today's poll must act on the promise to make noise in Canberra.

item.title: Australians with motor neurone disease fear losing priority pathway under NDIS reforms
item.description: Australians with MND who have rapidly changing needs fear they will lose a priority pathway to access the National Disability Insurance Scheme under Labor’s planned changes.

item.title: Some nannies working without superannuation due to 'discriminatory' law
item.description: Despite working full-time hours, some private domestic workers are going years without superannuation contributions. Now, industry stakeholders are calling for change.

item.title: Mother's Day complicated and full of love for kids in foster care
item.description: Jaharn grew up in foster care from two years of age. Now 19, he and his foster mum share their lessons of love.

item.title: Deaf and blind cricketers feel disrespected by changes to Cricket Australia's funding
item.description: Significant changes to how Cricket Australia funds Deaf, blind and intellectually disabled teams prompt concern from players and advocates.

item.title: Family of ousted Myanmar leader Aung San Suu Kyi call for 'proof of life'
item.description: Aung San Suu Kyi's son demands proof his mother is still alive, as Myanmar's military junta keeps tens of thousands of political prisoners behind bars.
